title: impatiens balsamina var. parusnathica
intro: Impatiens balsamina var. parusnathica, also known as the 'Garden Balsam' or 'Parusnath Balsam', is a variety of the Impatiens balsamina plant, a flowering plant native to India and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Impatiens balsamina var. parusnathica include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine.
